relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Judah went down from his brothers and turned aside into another story.
He joined himself to a Canaanite household.
Er was born.
Onan was born.
Shelah was born while Judah was at Chezib.
Tamar entered Judah’s house as the woman of Er.
Er’s evil brought his life to an end.
Onan was given the duty to raise seed for his brother.
He refused to build his brother’s line.
His refusal was judged as evil.
Tamar was sent back to her father’s house, promised Shelah but effectively withheld.
After Judah’s woman died, he went toward Timnah for shearing.
Tamar heard Judah was coming.
She changed garments and placed herself where Judah would have to face the duty denied her.
Judah saw but did not recognize her.
The unrecognized woman negotiated with the man who had withheld justice.
A pledge was required before the promised goat.
Seal, cord, and staff became the evidence that would later speak truth.
Tamar returned to widow’s garments carrying the hidden future.
Judah sent payment but could not retrieve the pledge.
No one knew the woman he sought.
The friend returned empty-handed.
Judah chose silence to avoid shame.
When Tamar’s pregnancy was exposed, Judah judged her harshly.
Tamar answered with recognition: whose seal, cords, and staff are these?
Judah recognized the truth and confessed: she is more righteous than I.
Tamar carried twins.
A first hand appeared and was marked with scarlet.
Perez broke through first, breach becoming name.
Zerah followed with the scarlet thread.
